title: Isoloma seemannii
taxonomy: Isoloma seemannii is classified under the family Hymenophyllaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as filmy ferns or air plants. This family is part of the larger division Pteridophyta, which includes all vascular plants that reproduce via spores. The order for Hymenophyllaceae is Hymenophyllales, which is an order of ferns that are primarily epiphytic or lithophytic. Within the family Hymenophyllaceae, Isoloma seemannii is placed in the genus Isoloma, which is a group of ferns that are native to tropical regions. The species Isoloma seemannii is further identified by its unique characteristics and distribution.

intro: Isoloma seemannii, also known as New Zealand pepper, is a species of plant in the family Cunoniaceae that is native to New Zealand. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation, and combat infections. The bioactive constituents of Isoloma seemannii include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
